title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: May 13, 2026 - Market Trends and Economic Highlights

items.headline: Wall Street's Record Run Halted by Tech Weakness and Inflation Fears
items.summary: U.S. equities experienced a mixed session as concerns over hotter-than-expected inflation data and weakness in technology and AI-related stocks tempered investor sentiment. The Dow managed a slight gain, while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite pulled back from recent highs. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East continued to keep oil prices elevated, adding to inflationary pressures.

items.statContext: Slipped 0.7% due to weak performance by technology bigwigs.

items.headline: SKK Holdings Soars on Acquisition News
items.summary: SKK Holdings Limited surged after signing a definitive asset purchase agreement to acquire drone assets from Rantizo, Inc., establishing itself as a publicly-traded operator of drone-based platforms.

items.headline: UnitedHealth Leads Dow Gainers
items.summary: UnitedHealth Group Inc. was a significant gainer within the Dow Jones Industrial Average.

items.headline: Intel Plummets Amid AI Stock Pullback
items.summary: Intel slumped as a broader pullback hit chip companies and other stocks linked to the artificial intelligence boom.

items.headline: Hims & Hers Misses Q1 Expectations
items.summary: Hims & Hers Health, Inc. experienced a sharp decline after reporting first-quarter earnings that missed analyst expectations.

items.headline: April Inflation Data Raises Rate Hike Expectations
items.summary: The U.S. consumer inflation accelerated to 3.8% in April, surpassing expectations of 3.7% and marking the highest level since May 2023. This hotter-than-expected data has redirected market expectations towards a potential Federal Reserve rate hike in early 2027. The Producer Price Index (PPI) was also released today, providing further insights into wholesale inflation.

items.headline: Semiconductors and Energy Lead, Tech and Discretionary Lag
items.summary: Sector performance showed divergence, with Semiconductors, Energy, Defense, and Industrials leading the charge. Conversely, the broader Technology sector, especially AI-related stocks, Communication Services, Consumer Staples, Commercial REITs, and Consumer Discretionary sectors experienced declines. This suggests a rotation into more defensive and cyclically-driven areas.

items.headline: Middle East Tensions Keep Oil Prices Elevated, Fueling Inflation Concerns
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East continue to disrupt tanker movements and keep oil prices high, with Brent crude around $107.77 per barrel. This situation is contributing to inflationary pressures and adds to the uncertainty in the economic outlook, a key concern highlighted by the Federal Reserve.

items.headline: Earnings Season Continues with NVIDIA, Walmart, BioNano Genomics, and Cisco
items.summary: Major companies continue to report earnings this week, with NVIDIA ($NVDA) and Walmart ($WMT) scheduled. BioNano Genomics ($BNGO) and Cisco ($CSCO) are also set to release their earnings reports today. Investors are closely watching these reports for insights into corporate performance amidst current macro trends.
